chooseResponse.q: What did you think of the lecture?
options: I can't wait to read more.

conversation.transcript:
Student: Hey, did you go to the art exhibit last night?
Advisor: Yes, I did! It was really impressive. Did you see the sculpture section?
Student: I missed that! What was so special about it?
Advisor: The artist used recycled materials to create amazing pieces. You should check it out!

questions.q: What did the student miss at the exhibit?
What materials did the artist use?
questions.explain: 藝術家使用了回收材料。

announcement.transcript: Attention students! The library will be closed for maintenance this weekend. Please make sure to return or renew your books before Friday evening. We apologize for any inconvenience and appreciate your understanding.

questions.q: Why will the library be closed?
questions.explain: 圖書館會因為維護而關閉。

questions.q: When should students return their books?
questions.explain: 學生應該在星期五晚上之前歸還書籍。

talk.transcript: Today, I want to discuss the concept of biodiversity and its importance to ecosystems. Biodiversity refers to the variety of life found on Earth, including the different species of plants, animals, and microorganisms. It plays a crucial role in maintaining the balance of ecosystems. High biodiversity helps ecosystems to be more resilient to changes, such as climate change or human impact. For instance, diverse ecosystems can recover more quickly from disturbances. Additionally, biodiversity contributes to human well-being by providing resources like food, medicine, and clean air. Protecting biodiversity is essential for a sustainable future.

questions.explain: 生物多樣性是指物種的多樣性。
生物多樣性有助於氣候調節。

questions.q: How does biodiversity affect human well-being?
questions.explain: 生物多樣性通過提供資源來影響人類福祉。

questions.q: What can diverse ecosystems do better?
questions.explain: 多樣的生態系統能更好地從擾動中恢復。
